target_area.description: Results Section 4.3 bridge from VLM stress evidence to material-effect mechanisms and static-gate claim boundary

change_summary: Changed the Section 4.3 opener from a generic audit transition to a mechanistic question: when evidence moves, which material channel moved it.
Kept the NVIDIA baseline comparison bounded to material bins with provenance rather than expanding the claim.
Compacted the static-gate boundary sentence so page 5 ends at a complete sentence and page 6 begins cleanly with Section 4.4.

observations: The first draft removed the page-5 large interword gap but introduced an awkward page-break hyphenation across 'selected-bin interpretation'.
The final wording ends the static-gate paragraph on page 5 with 'selected bins, not population-level rates.'
Page 6 now begins directly with Section 4.4; Figure 3 and its caption remain stable.

verification.command: rg -n "Overfull|Float too large|too large|LaTeX Warning: Float|Rerun to get|Warning:.*Label|Undefined references|multiply defined|Package lineno Warning" paper/venues/acl27/build/main.log || true

verification.evidence: ok=true; title and abstract match metadata; abstract_word_count=169
PDF text contains 'mechanistic question', 'material channel moved it', 'bounded provenance', and 'selected bins, not population-level rates'.

workspace_note: The repository had unrelated pre-existing modified paper and table files before this round. This evidence record only claims the round29 Section 4.3 bridge/static-gate wording polish plus the new record/evidence files.
